content:
# Revelation Chapter 15 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE, pages 288-289 | **Source Image:** REV_015_page_001.jpg, REV_015_page_002.jpg

1. And I saw another sign in the heaven, great and marvelous: seven angels having seven plagues, the last [ones], because in them was completed the wrath of the God.

2. And I saw as a sea of glass having been mixed with fire, and the [ones] conquering out of the beast and out of the image of it and out of the number of the name of it, standing upon the sea of glass, having harps of the God.

3. And they sing the song of Moses, the slave of the God, and the song of the Lamb, saying, Great and marvelous [are] the works of you, Lord the God the Almighty. Righteous and true [are] the ways of you, the King of the nations.

4. Who will not fear, Lord, and glorify the name of you? Because [you] alone [are] holy. Because all the nations will come and worship before you, because the righteous judgments of you were made manifest.

5. And after these [things] I saw, and the temple of the tent of the testimony in the heaven was opened.

6. And the seven angels, the [ones] having the seven plagues, came out of the temple, having been clothed [in] clean bright linen, and having been girded around the chests with golden girdles.

7. And one of the four living creatures gave to the seven angels seven golden bowls, full of the wrath of the God, the [one] living unto the ages of the ages.

8. And the temple was filled [with] smoke from the glory of the God and from the power of him. And no one was able to enter into the temple until the seven plagues of the seven angels were completed.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **15:2 — "out of the beast and out of the image of it and out of the number of the name of it":** The triple εκ construction is preserved literally. Sinaiticus includes "out of the number of the name of it" where some manuscripts have a shorter reading. The preposition εκ here means "away from" or "victorious over."

2. **15:3 — "King of the nations" (βασιλευ των εθνων):** Sinaiticus reads "King of the nations." Other manuscripts read "King of the ages" (αιωνων) or "King of the saints" (αγιων). The Sinaiticus reading εθνων is followed here.

3. **15:4 — "holy" (οσιος):** Sinaiticus reads οσιος ("holy/devout") rather than αγιος. The term οσιος carries the sense of inherent holiness or piety, distinct from αγιος which is more "set apart."

4. **15:6 — "clean bright linen" (λινον καθαρον λαμπρον):** Sinaiticus reads λινον ("linen"). A well-known variant in some witnesses reads λιθον ("stone"), which appears to be a scribal error. The Sinaiticus reading λινον is clearly the correct text.

5. **15:5 — "tent of the testimony":** The phrase τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου echoes the LXX designation of the Tabernacle from the Torah. EMET preserves the literal construction "tent of the testimony" rather than smoothing to "Tabernacle of the Testimony."

— EMET
